title: Fusha cross-builder conflict record
description: An explicit record of a DISAGREEMENT between two of the five builders (arbitrary-typing checker, source-addressed parser/checker, rich-hover flywheel, certification validator, curriculum/tutor route) over the same token. P2 SURFACES the conflict and routes it — it NEVER silently picks a side. The winning_source_of_truth is selected by a fixed precedence order (source-addressed certainty > heuristic; cert-validator gate > candidate gate; deterministic verdict > suggestion; qg-palette enum > segment-role; source-clean public_boundary > internal evidence), gate_required is the max of the two, and next_action routes to two-vote / human / scholar review (or, for a public-gloss-vs-internal-lattice conflict, the public gloss stands and the lattice merely flags an internal review). Dry-run: live_writes const 0; public fields source-clean. See parserplans/general-fusha-grammar-checker-p2/006-cross-builder-conflict-resolution.md.

conflict_loc.description: token ref (tok:N) or S:A:W where the builders disagree.

reason_for_disagreement.description: authored, source-clean explanation of the disagreement.

winning_source_of_truth.description: which side the precedence order selects to TRUST for the resolution (or 'neither' when both must yield to review). This is a source-of-truth CATEGORY, not necessarily an involved_builders value; use winner_evidence_index to dereference it to a reading.

winner_evidence_index.description: index into evidence_from_both_sides of the reading the winner corresponds to, or null when no single reading wins (winning_source_of_truth='neither' or the winner is a process/third party, not one of the readings' builders).

evidence_from_both_sides.description: the two (or more) readings, each source-clean: {builder, reading, gate}.

$comment: Hard FAIL conditions in tools/validate_cross_builder_conflict.py: a conflict must carry a winning_source_of_truth AND a next_action AND a gate_required AND a route_to; gate_required must be the max of the two readings' gates; a C5 (verdict vs suggestion) must select deterministic_verdict; a C4 (candidate vs cert gate) must select cert_validator; a heuristic reading may never be the winner over a source_addressed reading; no public field leaks; evidence_from_both_sides has >=2 entries; never auto_safe for an iʿrāb-sensitive conflict.
